Intraday post (23/Jan)

Weekly post:

PCE, BoJ rate decision | Weekly post (20-23/Jan)

US-EU conflict (geo context, developments since last year):

Board of Peace, Greenland, Hidden Left-tail and Kurtosis | Geopolitical Developments [FREE]

From Wednesday’s post:Here you see OEX/SPX vs its yearly and 6-monthly MAs:

The rotation is ongoing, and regarding the volatility of it in the context of sentiment, we can expect more to come.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

Speed profile is net negative. Customers are net short downside vol and long upside vol. Downside is pretty much protected, upside vvol is sold. The positioning concentrates more on defending the left-tail than gamma squeeze up.

Centroid is at 6852.56 kinda like a local line-in the sand for today into the PM session. Anyways, spot is expected to move from there, not pin.

Downside pivot is at 6828.72Target is 6820.40Market expects reversion from the downside level if it is hit, back up to the centroid then pin to 6850. But it prices a margin os error of ~6790 to the downside if vol would catch up. Below the centroid, the trend should show up in vol.(Left-tail scenario, like policy shitshow, target is 6780/6760 area and pin back towards 6780/6800)

Upside pivot is at 6907.10Target is 6944.01The upside pivot is the true line in the sand. Spot is either above or below with the highest probability, watch closely into the opening. Upside target is pinning, with margin of error of 6965For gamma-squeeze, only the AM session is ok, and vol should not be compressed at the pivot. Low probability (like ~16%), but target is 6985/90 then pin to 6980 (lowest prob), or revert back to the upside target.

The upside pivot should give us nice reversion opportunities, mostly during the AM session. Afternoon, it is sticky.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
